key_insight: ClinRAG-GRAPH combines imaging, clinical, and biopsy data with graph-based learning and LLM-guided retrieval to improve pre-treatment breast pCR prediction.
clinical_relevance: If validated, this approach could help stratify breast cancer patients before neoadjuvant chemotherapy and support more personalized treatment planning.
method: Builds an intra-patient clinical-prior graph from DCE-MRI, structured clinical variables, and pathology biomarkers.
Uses a prior-guided relation-aware GCN to learn multimodal representations.
Adds domain-adversarial learning and LLM-based subgraph retrieval to improve robustness and interpretability.
evidence_signal: The model reported AUCs of 0.815 on the internal test set and 0.774/0.712 on two external test sets across multicenter cohorts.
watch_item: Performance may still depend on imaging protocol consistency, site-specific data quality, and the reliability of retrieved historical cases for explanation.
paper_title: ClinRAG-GRAPH: Clinical-prior Retrieval-Augmented Graph Model with Domain Adversarial Learning for Breast pCR Prediction
